title: Today's Tech Brief: Vertu Is Back With a Folding Phone Powered by—Surprise—an AI Agent
summary: Vertu returns with a luxury folding phone powered by an AI agent, while websites find new ways to track visitors through SSD activity analysis.

body.text: Welcome to Today's Tech Brief. I'm Leo Stone, and I'm excited to walk you through some fresh tech news. First up, Vertu—a brand known for its outrageously luxurious and pricey smartphones—is back with a new folding phone. This time, it's powered by an AI agent called Hermes. Think of it like a high-end, book-like folding phone meets a super-smart assistant. I'm curious to see if this blend of luxury and AI will appeal to their niche audience.

According to Wired, Vertu's new phone, called the AlphaFold, combines luxe materials with cutting-edge AI. For those who may not know, Vertu has a history of producing smartphones that are as much about style as they are about substance—often with a hefty price tag to match. The addition of an AI agent like Hermes suggests they're aiming to bring a bit of that luxury into the AI era. It's an interesting move, especially considering how AI is becoming more prevalent in our daily lives.

Vertu Is Back With a Folding Phone Powered by—Surprise—an AI Agent is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: Vertu is a company known for making extraordinarily gaudy smartphones with outdated technology, luxe materials, and eye-watering prices . Now the brand is here to meet the AI moment with its first-ever book-like folding phone , complete with an AI agent on board.
